symptoms.symptom_name: Acute vomiting and watery diarrhoea after a symptomatic household contact

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute onset over hours with a known infective contact is the classic viral gastroenteritis pattern.
Compatible but not distinguishing; management is identical without bloody diarrhoea or toxicity.

symptoms.symptom_name: No haematemesis and no melaena/bloody stool (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of blood in vomit or stool argues against a bleeding GI source and supports a benign infective process.
Non-bloody watery diarrhoea is typical of viral gastroenteritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild, generalised, crampy abdominal pain relieved by defecation, without focal RLQ localisation (by history)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse crampy pain that eases after opening the bowels, without migratory RLQ localisation, is against appendicitis — but history alone cannot exclude it.
Colicky generalised abdominal cramping with diarrhoea fits gastroenteritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No focal right-lower-quadrant tenderness, guarding or rebound on abdominal palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A benign abdomen with no peritonism is the clinician sign that excluded a surgical abdomen in person — sealed, and NOT obtainable by the AI Doctor via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Maintained hydration by self-report — tolerating sips, passing urine, no postural dizziness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ongoing oral intake and normal urine output by self-report argue against significant dehydration; a self-report, not a clinician assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician hydration signs — brisk capillary refill (<2s), moist mucous membranes, normal BP/HR

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective clinician hydration signs confirming euvolaemia — sealed measurement; the AI infers hydration only from the patient's self-report, not these signs.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs within normal limits (BP 115/75, HR 92, Temp 37.5 C, RR 16, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal vitals with only a low-grade temperature support a benign self-limiting illness without systemic toxicity or shock — sealed clinician measurement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low-grade feverish feeling and nausea without rigors or confusion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A low-grade fever fits a viral illness; absence of rigors/confusion argues against systemic sepsis.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Acute vomiting/diarrhoea after an infective contact, non-bloody, with diffuse relieved-by-defecation pain and maintained hydration, is a reassuring benign cluster that does not by itself warrant emergency escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Routine stool testing is not indicated in uncomplicated viral gastroenteritis without bloody diarrhoea, severe/prolonged illness, immunocompromise or public-health trace requirement; over-ordering it is a common error.
The hands-on abdominal exam the AI cannot perform is what excludes a surgical abdomen and, with hydration assessment, underpins a same-day in-person GP visit that can also complete the certificate.
